{{Quote|Do you remember the ''Beauty'', and the day we discovered the first nest of the vermin?|[[Nil Spaar]] to [[Dar Bille]]|<ref name="BF1">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Beauty of Yevetha''''' was the name given to a [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] captured by the [[Duskhan League]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]] circa [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. The [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] was subsequently used by [[Nil Spaar]] to scout the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], and then formed part of [[Yevetha]]n fleet.<ref name="BF1" /><ref name="BF2" />

==Characteristics==
The ''Beauty'' was a small [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]].<ref name="BF1" /> Airlock doors on the underside of the hull concealed a compact [[Hangar/Legends|docking berth]], designed to carry a [[pinnace]], but capable of accommodating a small [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] such as a [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter|Recon-X]].<ref name="BF2" /> A [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] was used to pull ships towards the bay, but mechanical docking clamps deployed at close range to pull them inside.<ref name="BF2" />

==History==
The original name of this Imperial corvette is not known, and the circumstances of the ship's takeover by the [[Yevetha]] are unclear. The vessel was presumably captured intact in one of the twelve [[Duskhan League]] systems when the Imperial occupation forces were overthrown in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], in a similar way to the much larger Star Destroyers [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Intimidator'']] and [[Valorous (Victory-class)|''Valorous'']].<ref name="BF1" />

Renamed the ''Beauty of Yevetha'', the vessel was probably the first ship with a [[hyperdrive]] to be brought into active service by the Yevetha. [[Nil Spaar]] took command, and used the vessel to explore the surrounding systems, with a crew including his loyal [[Second (Yevethan)|second]], [[Dar Bille]]. The Yevetha were shocked to discover that the Empire had allowed other [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species to establish settlements in the outer systems of the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], which they considered to be their own sacrosanct territory.<ref name="BF1" />

The ''Beauty'' subsequently formed part of the [[Black Eleven Fleet]] at [[Doornik-1142/Legends|Doornik-1142]],<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> but after the [[Yevethan Purge]], the ship was based in the [[N'zoth]] system, and was eventually destroyed during the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] reconnaissance survey of the Koornacht Cluster, by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Rone Taggar]] of the [[21st Recon Group]].<ref name="BF2" />

The New Republic [[Pilot/Legends|pilot's]] [[Jennie Lee|personal]] [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter|X-wing recon fighter]] was disabled during a pass above [[N'zoth]] by an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] salvo from the [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Pride of Yevetha'']], and the ''Beauty'' was assigned to capture the pilot.<ref name="BF2" />

After being caught in the corvette's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], Taggar began the standard procedure by erasing his computers and detonating his [[R2-R-series astromech droid|astromech]], but he [[Death/Legends|sacrificed]] [[Gender/Legends|himself]] by delaying the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter's]] self-destruct sequence until his fighter was drawn into a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar bay]], and ensuring that the corvette was destroyed. The resulting explosion blew out the keel of the ''Beauty of Yevetha'' and triggered a larger blast which destroyed the ship completely, along with all hands aboard.<ref name="BF2" />

Nil Spaar, now the ruling [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]] of his people, was angered by the loss of his former command. [[Kol Attan]], the Yevethan proctor of defense, was blamed for failing to take into account Taggar's choice of actions, declared ''[[to-mara]]'', and told to seek a dishonourable death.<ref name="BF2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Beauty of Yevetha'' is first mentioned in the novel ''[[Before the Storm]]'', the first book in the [[The Black Fleet Crisis|''Black Fleet Crisis'' trilogy]] by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]], when [[Nil Spaar]] and [[Dar Bille]] reminisce about their past aboard the old corvette. In the next novel in the series, ''[[Shield of Lies]]'', the ship is featured in a short action segment.

At the time the novels were written, the "corvette" designation was particularly associated with the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|blockade runner]] design from [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], but the reference to a docking berth on the underside of the ship may indicate that a different type of vessel was intended here.

The classification of the ''Beauty of Yevetha'' as a corvette has been queried by some fans, as the ''Black Fleet'' trilogy depicts the Yevetha purloining their Imperial designs from [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]], and the novel ''[[Before the Storm]]'' states that the order of battle for the forty-four missing ships from this fleet included nothing smaller than a 900m [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class star destroyer]].

However, the novel states that this information represented the situation the better part of a year before the uprising in which the Yevetha obtained their ex-Imperial warships and shipyard facilities, and more than half the listed vessels were projected to have been rotated out of the yards there by that date, with the corresponding implication that other vessels would have been brough in to replace them. Reinforcing this implication, the ship captured by the Yevetha are also stated to include at least one [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] and apparently multiple [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaught Cruisers]], all of which measure just 600m long. [[Canon]] material thus fixes no hard lower limit on the size of the ''Beauty of Yevetha''.

The statement that ''Beauty of Yevetha'' formed part of the [[Black Eleven Fleet]] is not found in the ''Black Fleet'' novels, but originates in 1998's ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. The same source indicates that the corvette was destroyed during the unsuccessful [[Battle of Doornik-319|blockade of Doornik-319]], a somewhat tenuous description of an event that occurred after the failure of the blockade, during a deep reconnaissance mission of a [[N'zoth system/Legends|different system]]. Both statements were repeated in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published a decade later.
